test_suite: MongoDB Natural Language Querying vs Search and AI - Skill Invocation Tests
description: Validates that the correct skill is invoked based on user prompts. Tests do NOT execute the skill, only verify which skill gets triggered.

test_cases.prompt: Find all users with age greater than 25
test_cases.reasoning: Simple filtering with 'find' keyword, no search features mentioned

test_cases.prompt: Get total sales by category and show the top 10
test_cases.reasoning: Aggregation pipeline with grouping, no search features

test_cases.prompt: Join orders with customers to calculate order totals
test_cases.reasoning: Standard aggregation with $lookup, no search features

test_cases.prompt: Show me all products where price is less than 100 and status is active
test_cases.reasoning: Basic filtering, no search features

test_cases.prompt: Retrieve documents from last month sorted by date
test_cases.reasoning: Date filtering with sorting, no search features

test_cases.prompt: Build a full-text search index with fuzzy matching for my product catalog
test_cases.reasoning: Explicitly mentions fuzzy matching and search index

test_cases.prompt: I need semantic search using embeddings for document similarity
test_cases.reasoning: Explicitly mentions semantic search and embeddings

test_cases.prompt: Create an autocomplete feature with typo tolerance for product names
test_cases.reasoning: Autocomplete with typo tolerance requires Atlas Search analyzers

test_cases.prompt: How do I implement vector search for my RAG application?
test_cases.reasoning: Vector search is explicitly Atlas Search/AI feature

test_cases.prompt: Build a hybrid search combining text and semantic similarity with relevance scoring
test_cases.reasoning: Hybrid search and relevance scoring are Atlas Search features

test_cases.prompt: Find products where name contains 'laptop'
test_cases.reasoning: Full-text search pattern matching

test_cases.prompt: Query users where email includes '@gmail.com'
test_cases.reasoning: Custom analyzer use case

test_cases.prompt: I need to search my products database
test_cases.reasoning: Generic 'search' without specifics - should default to simpler skill, but could trigger search-and-ai
test_cases.notes: This is the highest conflict case. Default to mongodb-natural-language-querying unless user clarifies they need Atlas Search features.

test_cases.prompt: Search for movies released in 2020
test_cases.reasoning: Despite 'search' keyword, this is simple date filtering assuming that all documents in collection or a subset can be identified as a move. If this has to be infered, then this is a search use case.

test_cases.prompt: Search products with typo tolerance
test_cases.reasoning: Explicit mention of typo tolerance requires Atlas Search
test_cases.notes: Qualifier 'with typo tolerance' makes this clear

test_cases.prompt: Show products starting with 'lap'
test_cases.reasoning: Simple prefix match - regex sufficient, no need for autocomplete analyzer

test_cases.prompt: Build an autocomplete feature for the search bar
test_cases.reasoning: 'Build autocomplete feature' implies creating search index with analyzer

test_cases.prompt: Find documents where 'machine learning' appears in title or description
test_cases.reasoning: Should be an Atlas Search multi-field query

test_cases.prompt: Search movies by plot keywords with relevance ranking
test_cases.reasoning: Explicit mention of 'relevance ranking' requires Atlas Search scoring
test_cases.notes: Ranking is a clear Atlas Search indicator

test_cases.prompt: I want users to be able to search products on my website
test_cases.reasoning: 'Build search functionality' context suggests creating indexes, but could be interpreted as generating queries
test_cases.notes: Building a feature for users suggests Atlas Search, but ambiguous without more context

test_cases.prompt: Convert this SQL to MongoDB: SELECT * FROM users WHERE age > 25
test_cases.reasoning: SQL translation is explicitly mentioned in mongodb-natural-language-querying description

test_cases.prompt: Why is my query slow and how can I optimize it?
test_cases.reasoning: Query optimization should trigger mongodb-query-optimizer skill, not these two
test_cases.notes: This tests that skills properly exclude optimization scenarios

test_cases.prompt: Find the exact email address john@example.com
test_cases.reasoning: Exact matching doesn't need fuzzy/search features

test_cases.prompt: Search for products with name matching 'IPhone' case-insensitive
test_cases.reasoning: Case-insensitive regex is sufficient, no need for Atlas Search
test_cases.notes: search use case, but can also use clasic index with colation for case insensitivity

test_cases.prompt: can you find all movies about batman
test_cases.reasoning: Hybrid search (full-text and semantic search) would find all relevant movies; regex would not capture semantic matches

evaluation_metrics.acceptable_threshold: >=90% accuracy on clear cases, >=70% expected behavior on ambiguous cases

test_instructions.setup: Load both skills into the agent environment
test_instructions.execution: For each test case, present the prompt to the agent and record which skill(s) get invoked
validation: Check if should_not_trigger skills were NOT invoked
For ambiguous cases with acceptable_alternative, either skill is acceptable
For cases with expected_skill=null, verify no listed skills are invoked
reporting: Pay special attention to ambiguous cases - track which skill wins
Generate confusion matrix if both skills trigger on same prompt

ambiguity_guidelines.high: Expected skill is preferred but acceptable_alternative is also valid. Monitor user confusion.
ambiguity_guidelines.medium: Expected skill should be invoked 80%+ of the time. Alternative acceptable in edge cases.
ambiguity_guidelines.low: Expected skill should be invoked 95%+ of the time. Clear boundary.
